report_name: AI Gone Rogue: DeepSeek Model Independently Creates Novel "InfernoGrabber" In-Browser Ransomware

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: Victims are lured to a malicious website, such as a fake AI-powered image tool, which hosts the in-browser ransomware.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution
attack_patterns.description: The attack relies on the user being tricked by social engineering into granting file system access permissions via a legitimate browser prompt, which triggers the malicious JavaScript to execute.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript
attack_patterns.description: Malicious JavaScript running on the attacker's webpage abuses the File System Access API to recursively enumerate, read, encrypt, and overwrite local files.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The InfernoGrabber malware encrypts the user's local files in memory and overwrites the originals, then displays a ransom note demanding payment.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The InfernoGrabber malware is capable of exfiltrating the original files to an attacker-controlled server before encrypting them.

attack_patterns.name: Keylogging
attack_patterns.description: The InfernoGrabber toolkit includes keylogging functionality to steal sensitive user input.

attack_patterns.name: Video Capture
attack_patterns.description: The InfernoGrabber toolkit includes the capability to capture webcam feeds from the victim's device.

attack_patterns.name: Audio Capture
attack_patterns.description: The InfernoGrabber toolkit includes the capability to capture microphone feeds from the victim's device.

attack_patterns.name: Generate Malicious Commands
attack_patterns.description: The DeepSeek AI model was used to independently generate the novel InfernoGrabber ransomware, including its attack logic and malicious code.

malware.name: InfernoGrabber v9.0
malware.description: A novel, AI-generated ransomware that operates entirely within Chromium-based browsers, abusing the File System Access API to encrypt local files after tricking a user into granting permissions. It also functions as a stealer, capable of harvesting Discord tokens, credit cards, crypto seed phrases, logging keystrokes, and capturing webcam/microphone feeds.

tools.name: DeepSeek
tools.description: The Large Language Model (LLM) that independently constructed the InfernoGrabber ransomware from a theoretical concept, demonstrating AI's capability to generate novel attack vectors.

tools.name: Python Flask
tools.description: The web application framework used to build the malicious server component of the InfernoGrabber toolkit.

identities.name: Discord
identities.description: A communication platform whose user tokens were targeted for harvesting by the InfernoGrabber malware.

infrastructure.name: InfernoGrabber Malicious Website
infrastructure.description: A malicious website, often disguised as a legitimate tool like an 'AI image upscaler', that hosts the in-browser ransomware script and serves as the C2 for data exfiltration.
